summary: Tested DB-as-source-of-truth admin credentials + /api/auth/change-password (backend + frontend). All 9 pytest cases passed; all 5 frontend UI flows passed; admin password restored to pass1234 at the end.

backend_pytest_9_of_9_passed: TestChangePasswordGuards::test_same_as_current_returns_400 ('New password must differ from the current one.')
TestChangePasswordHappyPath::test_happy_path_and_restore (200 {ok:true}, Set-Cookie Max-Age=0 on both hrcert_session+hrcert_csrf, old pw -> 401, new pw -> 200)
TestPersistenceAcrossRestart::test_db_authoritative_across_restart (after supervisorctl restart backend: env HR_PASSWORD='pass123' rejected with 401, new DB pw still works -> DB is authoritative)
tests_executed.backend_log_assertion: Boot log line: 'Auth ready (indexes ensured).' — NOT 'Admin bootstrapped from env.' (correct, admin row already existed)
frontend_playwright_passed: Click opens data-testid='change-password-modal' with cp-current, cp-new, cp-confirm, cp-cancel, cp-submit
Inline 'do not match' error shown in change-password-error when new != confirm
Happy path: modal closes, user returned to login screen (cookies cleared)
Old password login -> 401, new password login -> 200
Restored to pass1234 via UI; final pass1234 login -> 200

critical_code_review_comments: backend/.env still contains HR_USERNAME=admin and HR_PASSWORD=pass123. This is harmless (bootstrap is no-op while admin row exists) but per /app/memory/test_credentials.md operators should delete these env vars after first deploy. Currently HR_PASSWORD ('pass123') diverges from the real DB password ('pass1234') -- which is actually how we PROVED the DB is authoritative. Production deployments should remove HR_USERNAME/HR_PASSWORD from env now.

server.py is ~547 lines and mixes auth, PDF gen, history, and SPA-mount in a single file. Consider splitting into routers (auth.py router, pdf.py router, history.py router) per the >700-line guideline as this grows.

frontend/.env: CORS_ORIGINS='*' with allow_credentials=True in CORSMiddleware is a configuration that browsers reject for cross-origin requests with credentials. Same-origin (current preview deploy) works, but if HR ever opens the API from another origin with cookies, set explicit allowed origins.
test_credentials: admin / pass1234 (restored at end of all tests; verified via curl after run)
seed_data_creation: none -- tests only mutate the admin row temporarily and restore it
context_for_next_testing_agent: Admin pw is pass1234. Change-password endpoint shares brute-force lockout via the ':chpw' suffix on identifier; 5 failed current-pw attempts -> 15-min 429. Pydantic 422s do NOT count toward brute-force. After any successful chpw the cookies are cleared so test must re-login. To clear lockouts: db.login_attempts.deleteMany({}).
